seo_title: Current Time in the Cotswolds — GMT/BST (UTC+0/+1)
seo_description: Current time in the Cotswolds, UK. Live GMT/BST clock — UTC+0 standard, UTC+1 British Summer Time. Cotswolds time vs New York, London, Paris, Dublin.

faq.question: What time is it in the Cotswolds right now?
faq.answer: The Cotswolds operates on Greenwich Mean Time (GMT/UTC+0) during winter and British Summer Time (BST/UTC+1) from late March to late October. The Cotswolds spans parts of Gloucestershire, Oxfordshire, Warwickshire, and Worcestershire — all within the UK national time zone. To see the exact current time in the Cotswolds with live seconds, use our real-time clock above.

faq.question: What time zone are the Cotswolds in?
faq.answer: The Cotswolds is in the Greenwich Mean Time zone (GMT/UTC+0 in winter, BST/UTC+1 in summer), the same as all of England, Wales, and Scotland. The region covers an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty (AONB) across six counties including Gloucestershire and Oxfordshire. There is no separate Cotswolds time zone — it follows UK national time, identical to London, Manchester, and Edinburgh.

faq.question: Are the Cotswolds in the same time zone as London?
faq.answer: Yes. The Cotswolds is in exactly the same time zone as London — GMT in winter and BST (UTC+1) in summer. Villages like Bourton-on-the-Water, Burford, Chipping Campden, and Stow-on-the-Wold all share London's time. The Cotswolds is approximately 1.5–2 hours from London by road or train, with no time change involved.

faq.question: Does the Cotswolds observe Daylight Saving Time?
faq.answer: Yes. The Cotswolds, as part of the UK, observes British Summer Time (BST). Clocks spring forward one hour on the last Sunday in March at 1:00 AM GMT (becoming 2:00 AM BST/UTC+1), and fall back one hour on the last Sunday in October at 2:00 AM BST (becoming 1:00 AM GMT/UTC+0). The long BST summer evenings — with sunset sometimes as late as 9:15 PM — are highly prized for evening walks and village pub gardens.

faq.question: What is the time difference between the Cotswolds and New York?
faq.answer: The Cotswolds is 5 hours ahead of New York during US Eastern Standard Time (EST/UTC-5), and 5 hours ahead during EDT (UTC-4) as well, since UK DST transitions occur around the same time as US DST. When it is 9:00 AM in New York, it is 2:00 PM in the Cotswolds. This consistent 5-hour gap makes it easy for American visitors and property owners to coordinate with Cotswolds hotels, estate agents, and tour operators.

faq.question: What is the time difference between the Cotswolds and Paris?
faq.answer: The Cotswolds (GMT/BST) is always 1 hour behind Paris (CET/CEST). When it is 12:00 PM in Paris, it is 11:00 AM in the Cotswolds. Both the UK and France observe DST on the same Sunday in March and October, so the 1-hour difference is maintained year-round. Many French visitors drive through the Channel Tunnel and add just 1 hour to their watches on arrival.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit the Cotswolds?
faq.answer: The best time to visit the Cotswolds is May–September for warm weather and the longest daylight hours (BST/UTC+1). June and July are peak season, with the famous honey-stone villages and gardens at their most picturesque. Spring (April–May) brings bluebell woods and fewer crowds. Autumn (September–October) offers golden foliage and harvest festivals. Christmas markets in towns like Bourton-on-the-Water and Moreton-in-Marsh (late November–December) draw visitors who enjoy winter charm, though some attractions operate reduced hours.

faq.question: What are typical opening hours for Cotswolds attractions?
faq.answer: Most Cotswolds visitor attractions, stately homes, and gardens open 10:00 AM–5:00 PM during the main season (April–October), with last entry typically 30–60 minutes before closing. National Trust properties such as Hidcote Garden and Snowshill Manor generally open 10:00 AM–5:00 PM Wednesday–Monday. Village pubs in the Cotswolds typically open 11:00 AM–11:00 PM, with food service 12:00 PM–2:30 PM and 6:00 PM–9:00 PM. Many attractions close entirely from November–March or open only at weekends.

content_blocks.title: Cotswolds Time Zone — GMT/BST, Same as London
content_blocks.content: The Cotswolds, England's most visited rural destination, operates on Greenwich Mean Time (GMT/UTC+0) in winter and British Summer Time (BST/UTC+1) from the last Sunday in March to the last Sunday in October. The region — designated an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty (AONB) spanning over 2,000 square kilometres — falls within the same UK national time zone as London, Birmingham, and Oxford. There is no time difference between Chipping Campden, Bourton-on-the-Water, or Broadway and London at any point in the year. The Cotswolds is 5 hours ahead of New York (EST), 1 hour behind Paris (CET), and shares the clock with Dublin and Lisbon in winter.

content_blocks.title: The Cotswolds: England's Honey-Stone Heartland
content_blocks.content: The Cotswolds is England's largest Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty, celebrated for its rolling limestone hills, dry-stone walls, and perfectly preserved medieval villages built from the distinctive golden-yellow Cotswold stone. The region spans six counties including Gloucestershire, Oxfordshire, Wiltshire, and Warwickshire. Key villages include Bourton-on-the-Water (the 'Venice of the Cotswolds'), Burford, Bibury (home to Arlington Row, one of England's most photographed streets), Chipping Campden, and Stow-on-the-Wold. The Cotswolds has served as a filming location for Downton Abbey (Bampton), Bridgerton (Castle Howard, Basildon Park), and numerous period productions. American tourists constitute the largest international visitor group, drawn by the region's quintessentially English character and ancestral connections.

content_blocks.title: Visiting the Cotswolds from Overseas — Practical Time Guide
content_blocks.content: For international visitors, the Cotswolds is easily reached from London Heathrow or London Paddington station. From Paddington, trains reach Moreton-in-Marsh in approximately 1 hour 40 minutes and Kemble (for Cirencester) in about 1 hour. The Cotswolds is in the GMT/BST zone — identical to London — so no clock adjustment is needed on arrival from the UK capital. From New York (EST), the Cotswolds is 5 hours ahead year-round: an 8:00 AM New York departure reaches the Cotswolds at 9:00 PM same day. From Sydney (AEST/UTC+10), the Cotswolds is 10 hours behind in UK winter, making early Sydney mornings the practical window for booking Cotswolds accommodation. The UK country code is +44; Cirencester (a common Cotswolds base) uses the 01285 area code.

timezone_facts.full_name: Greenwich Mean Time / British Summer Time
timezone_facts.dst_start: Last Sunday in March at 1:00 AM GMT
timezone_facts.dst_end: Last Sunday in October at 2:00 AM BST
